keyMappings:
# --- Navigation & Scrolling ---
# Native j/k works. Let's map half-page scrolls to match modern configs
map <c-d> scrollPageDown
map <c-u> scrollPageUp

# --- Tab Management (LazyVim Style: Bufferline behavior) ---
# Cycle tabs with H and L (like switching buffers via [b / ]b)
# Note: We unmap H and L from native goBack/goForward first so they can be re-bound cleanly.
unmap H
unmap L
map L nextTab
map H previousTab

# Create/Close Tabs (LazyVim window/buffer bindings)
map <space>fn createTab
map <space>bd removeTab
map u restoreTab

# Move tabs around
map <space>bl moveTabLeft
map <space>br moveTabRight

# --- URL & Clipboard Control ---
# Cmd+Shift+C to copy URL (Meta key 'm' handles Cmd on macOS)
map <m-C> copyCurrentUrl

# Yank / Paste bindings
map yf LinkHints.activateModeToCopyLinkUrl
map p openCopiedUrlInCurrentTab
map P openCopiedUrlInNewTab

# --- History & Navigation ---
# Jump backward/forward in tab history (Standard Vim jump list binds)
map <c-o> goBack
map <c-i> goForward

# --- Search & Link Interaction (Telescope / FZF-Lua Style) ---
# Space-Space launches the global search (Vomnibar)
map <space><space> Vomnibar.activate
map <space>ff Vomnibar.activateTabSelection
map <space>fb Vomnibar.activateBookmarks

# Link Hints (f for current tab, F for background)
map f LinkHints.activateMode
map F LinkHints.activateModeToOpenInNewTab
